avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already exist in the early morning. Increase in danger in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Moist and wet avalanches can be released by people, especially on very steep west, north and east facing slopes below approximately 2500 m. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be only a slight increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Individual natural avalanches are possible. Especially on steep shady slopes avalanches can be released in the weakly bonded old snow and reach large size in isolated cases.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. Such avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack below approximately 3000 m. The high humditiy will give rise in the afternoon to a loss of strength within the snowpack also on steep shady slopes.
Steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.

tendency.highlights: The backcountry touring conditions remain spring-like. The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will soften quickly. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches.

bulletin.highlights: Natural avalanches are to be expected as the day progresses.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation. Wind slabs in high Alpine regions.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In the early morning as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be a rapid increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Avalanches can in some places be released by a single winter sport participant. On very steep sunny slopes small and medium-sized moist loose snow avalanches are to be expected as the moisture increases. Avalanches can release the saturated snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases, in the regions exposed to a lot of new snow especially.
Wet slab avalanches are to be expected in the afternoon, in particular on steep shady slopes between approximately 2200 and 2600 m. Especially here avalanches can be released in the weakly bonded old snow and reach large size in some cases. This also applies in isolated cases in highly frequented off-piste terrain. In steep gullies the avalanches can reach areas without any snow cover.

The fresh wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases, in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls in high Alpine regions. Avalanches can in very isolated cases penetrate deep layers and reach large size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack below approximately 3000 m. The high humditiy will give rise in the afternoon to a loss of strength within the snowpack also on steep shady slopes.
West, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m: Towards its base, the snowpack consists of faceted crystals.
10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Monday above approximately 2500 m. In high Alpine regions mostly small wind slabs formed.

tendency.highlights: The backcountry touring conditions remain spring-like. The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will soften quickly. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already exist in the early morning. Increase in danger in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Moist and wet avalanches can be released by people, especially on very steep west, north and east facing slopes below approximately 2500 m. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be only a slight increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Individual natural avalanches are possible. Especially on steep shady slopes avalanches can be released in the weakly bonded old snow and reach large size in isolated cases.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. Such avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable.
In addition the fresh wind slabs are prone to triggering in some cases, in the regions exposed to a lot of new snow especially on steep shady slopes. On extremely steep sunny slopes small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist loose snow avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation, in the regions exposed to a lot of new snow especially.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack below approximately 3000 m. The high humditiy will give rise in the afternoon to a loss of strength within the snowpack also on steep shady slopes.
Steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.

tendency.highlights: The backcountry touring conditions remain spring-like. The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will soften quickly. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches.

bulletin.highlights: Natural avalanches are to be expected in the afternoon.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already exist in the early morning. Increase in danger in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In the early morning as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be a rapid increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Most and wet avalanches can in some places be released by a single winter sport participant. Individual mostly small loose snow avalanches are to be expected. Caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep slopes below approximately 2500 m, as well as on very steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes.
As the penetration by moisture increases the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses. More frequent wet slab avalanches are to be expected in the afternoon. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep shady slopes between approximately 2200 and 2600 m. Especially here wet avalanches can be released in the weakly bonded old snow and reach large size. This also applies in isolated cases in highly frequented off-piste terrain. In steep gullies the avalanches can reach areas without any snow cover.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. Such avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. The small wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence on near-ridge shady slopes in high Alpine regions.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night. For this reason the snowpack will only just freeze. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack below approximately 3000 m. The high humditiy will give rise in the afternoon to a loss of strength within the snowpack also on steep shady slopes.
West, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m: Towards its base, the snowpack consists of faceted crystals.

tendency.highlights: The backcountry touring conditions remain spring-like. The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will soften quickly. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an appreciable increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Natural wet avalanches are still to be expected.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In regions exposed to heavier precipitation the avalanche prone locations are more widespread.
avalancheActivity.comment: Wet and gliding avalanches are possible. The avalanche danger should be investigated very thoroughly in the relevant locality. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Places where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially precarious. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack. Gliding avalanches can also occur.

snowpackStructure.comment: The weather conditions will give rise to thorough wetting of the snowpack over a wide area. Snow cover and snow depth vary greatly depending on exposure and elevation. At low and intermediate altitudes a little snow is lying on south facing slopes. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be partly cloudy.

avalancheActivity.highlights: New snow and wind slabs at elevated altitudes. In particular, however, the danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase as the day progresses.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Towards its surface, the snowpack is unstable.

Above approximately 2200 m: As a consequence of new snow and northeasterly wind, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed in the last two days adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas. Avalanches can be released in near-surface layers and reach large size in particular on very steep shady slopes.

In particular on very steep grassy slopes and on rocky slopes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large wet avalanches are possible as the day progresses as the moisture increases.

Naturally triggered avalanches and penetration of the old snow cover indicate the significant danger.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 40 to 60 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell in the last two days above approximately 2000 m. Yesterday above approximately 2500 m numerous small and medium-sized avalanches were observed. Several occasionally large powder avalanches have been released at high altitude.
A clear night: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust, this also applies at low and intermediate altitudes.

Shady slopes as well as high altitudes: Over a wide area new snow and wind slabs are lying on a hard crust.

Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to gradual moistening of the snowpack. This applies in particular on steep east, south and west facing slopes below approximately 2700 m, as well as on north facing slopes below approximately 2400 m.

Below approximately 2500 m no snow is lying on south facing slopes.

The snowpack is well consolidated and its surface has a melt-freeze crust. This applies especially in all aspects below approximately 2300 m.

tendency.highlights:
The weather will be mild. A clear night will be followed by favourable conditions generally, but the danger of wet and gliding avalanches will increase later.

As a consequence of highly fluctuating temperatures and solar radiation the snow drift accumulations will stabilise during the next few days.

region.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The conditions are generally favourable for ski touring. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.
Locally, the night may be cloudy; in that case, the avalanche danger will already be 2—moderate by morning because The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will already be soft in the early morning. And so conditions in the mid-mountain range will already be somewhat unfavourable.
In the late morning the natural activity of moist and wet avalanches will gradually increase. At low and intermediate altitudes and on very steep slopes small and medium-sized moist snow slides and avalanches are to be expected.

As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes moderate southeasterly wind, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed. They have formed at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Colder than expected: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust. 20 to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Sunday above approximately 2300 m. New snow and wind slabs are lying on a wet old snowpack.
At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind. Below approximately 1900 m hardly any snow is lying.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation more moist and wet avalanches are possible. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to settling of the snowpack. Backcountry tours and ascents to alpine cabins should be concluded timely.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding snow require caution. Large and, in isolated cases, very large wet slab avalanches are possible in some places.
avalancheActivity.comment: In all aspects medium-sized and large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of the rain. As a consequence of the rain there will be an increase in the danger of gliding avalanches and wet snow slides. On wind-loaded slopes the avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size. Great caution and restraint are recommended. Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early.

snowpackStructure.comment: Various wind slab layers are lying mostly on a moist old snowpack. In particular, however, sometimes large wind slabs formed in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and generally in the high Alpine regions. The spring-like weather conditions gave rise to thorough wetting of the snowpack. As a consequence of the rain the prevalence and size of these avalanche prone locations will increase.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow at intermediate altitudes. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow in the high Alpine regions.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of the rain, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes below approximately 2200 m. From origins in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place natural avalanches are possible, even large ones in isolated cases.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2200 m. Such avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable.

In addition the fresh wind slabs in high Alpine regions are capable of being triggered in isolated cases. Wind slabs are mostly small.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Above approximately 2000 m snow has fallen over a wide area. Below approximately 1800 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying. At higher altitudes there are 30 to 80 cm of snow, and even as much as 150 cm in some localities.

The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night and will already be soft in the early morning. The rain will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2200 m. As a consequence of a strong wind, small wind slabs will form in high Alpine regions.

Steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.

tendency.highlights: On Wednesday it will be mostly sunny. Decrease in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of the ceasing of precipitation.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of wet and gliding avalanches will already exist in the early morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will already be soft in the early morning, in the event of an overcast night, also at intermediate and high altitudes.

In the late morning the natural activity of moist and wet avalanches will rapidly increase.
At low and intermediate altitudes and on very steep grassy slopes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible as the penetration by moisture increases.

The wet fresh snow and the small wind slabs formed during the snowfall can be released by a single winter sport participant. Naturally triggered avalanches and penetration of the old snow cover indicate the existence of a weak snowack.

Backcountry tours and ascents to alpine cabins should be started and concluded very early.

snowpackStructure.comment:
5 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell in the last two days above approximately 2000 m.

The sleet gave rise on Tuesday to extreme moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2200 m. In some places relatively hard layers of snow are lying on a wet old snowpack.

tendency.highlights:
The weather will be very mild.
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will soften quickly.
The spring-like weather conditions from the late morning will give rise to a loss of strength within the snowpack over a wide area.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.
Locally, the night may be cloudy; in that case, the avalanche danger will already be 2—moderate by morning because The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will already be soft in the early morning. And so conditions in the mid-mountain range will already be somewhat unfavourable.
In the late morning the natural activity of moist and wet avalanches will gradually increase. At low and intermediate altitudes and on very steep slopes small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist snow slides and avalanches are to be expected.
The conditions are mostly favourable for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities.
As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes moderate southeasterly wind, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed. They have formed at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions. The conditions are generally favourable for ski touring.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Colder than expected: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust. New snow and wind slabs are lying on a wet old snowpack.
At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind. Below approximately 1900 m hardly any snow is lying.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation more moist and wet avalanches are possible. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to settling of the snowpack. Backcountry tours and ascents to alpine cabins should be concluded timely.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh snow and the mostly small wind slabs formed during the snowfall can be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies in particular on very steep slopes at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions, as well as at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

In particular on very steep slopes small and medium-sized gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible as the penetration by moisture increases.

Backcountry tours and ascents to alpine cabins should be started early and concluded timely.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some regions 15 to 30 cm of snow, but less in some localities, fell in the last two days above approximately 2100 m.
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will already soften in the late morning, this also applies at low and intermediate altitudes. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise from early morning to increasing moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2300 m. This applies in particular on steep east, south and west facing slopes below approximately 2700 m, as well as on north facing slopes below approximately 2400 m.

Below approximately 2200 m no snow is lying on south facing slopes.

The snowpack is well consolidated and its surface has a melt-freeze crust, especially on shady slopes above approximately 1800 m, as well as in all aspects below approximately 2300 m.

tendency.highlights:
The weather will be mild.
A clear night will be followed by favourable conditions generally, but the danger of wet and gliding avalanches will increase later.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
The weather will be partly cloudy over a wide area.
Warming.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m.
As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m.
Medium-sized avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack cooled hardly at all during the overcast night and will already be soft in the early morning.
Above approximately 2400 m snow fell in the last two days over a wide area.
As a consequence of wind, mostly shallow wind slabs formed in high Alpine regions.
Steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.
Below approximately 1800 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In some localities up to 20 cm of snow, but less in some localities, has fallen above approximately 2600 m. Up to 2300 m rain has fallen in some localities.
The avalanche prone locations are prevalent and are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility. The avalanche prone locations for wet avalanches are to be found in particular on cut slopes below approximately 2500 m. From starting zones that have retained some snow in particular the wet avalanches can reach large size. The new snow is to be evaluated with care and prudence in all aspects above approximately 2500 m. In particular on very steep slopes and at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized avalanches are possible. Bases of rock walls are especially dangerous.
As a consequence of new snow and a strong southeasterly wind, large surface-area wind slabs formed especially above approximately 2500 m. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls in northwest to north to northeast facing aspects above approximately 2500 m. Transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially dangerous. Avalanches can be triggered in the old snowpack and reach medium size.
In some regions increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Precarious weak layers exist in the snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
The sleet gave rise to a loss of strength within the snowpack over a wide area in particular on steep slopes below approximately 2200 m.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow represents the main danger. As a consequence of the rain natural wet avalanches are possible.
avalancheActivity.comment: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already be elevated in the early morning. As a consequence of the rain, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes. Only isolated natural avalanches are possible, even medium-sized ones.

snowpackStructure.comment: The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little and will already be soft in the early morning. Up to high altitudes rain will fall. The rain will give rise to a loss of strength within the snowpack especially on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: Decrease in danger of wet avalanches as the precipitation eases.

avalancheActivity.comment: Only isolated wet avalanches are possible. Caution is to be exercised on extremely steep shady slopes. In very isolated cases avalanches are medium-sized.

snowpackStructure.comment: The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little and will already be soft in the early morning.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
The weather will be partly cloudy over a wide area.
Warming.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The surface of the snowpack is not frozen and will already be soft in the early morning.
In isolated cases the avalanches are small.

snowpackStructure.comment: As a consequence of mild temperatures a sometimes unfavourable avalanche situation will develop.
